title: mcp-stage cassette entry (v1)
description: A cassette is a .jsonl file. Each line is one entry validating against this schema. Line 1 MUST be a `meta` entry. Request/response pairs are stored as a single `exchange` entry (joined by the recorder) so replay matching never has to re-correlate JSON-RPC ids. Unpaired messages (notifications, cancelled requests) get their own entry types.

id.description: Stable upstream id; matches `upstreams[].id` in a stage definition so one cassette is portable across stage configs.

protocol_version.description: MCP protocol version negotiated at initialize.

capabilities.description: Server capabilities from initialize result, verbatim. Replay serves these instead of a live handshake.

redacted.description: True if a redaction pass has run (v1.x feature; field reserved now).

correlation.description: Optional fleet-scale correlation metadata for indexing/searching across many cassettes (interop-friendly with Agent VCR's session/endpoint/agent tagging). Never used by replay matching or fork seeding.

agent_id.description: Identity of the agent that drove this session.

endpoint_id.description: Logical endpoint/deployment this cassette was recorded against (distinct from upstream.url, which may be shared).

run_id.description: Eval/CI run identifier for correlating N parallel cassettes from one run.

seq.description: Monotonic per-cassette ordinal. Replay's `call #N` addressing and scenario `calls` matchers use this ordering.

session_id.description: mcp-stage session id (not the transport's Mcp-Session-Id, which is recorded under transport).

ts.description: Wall-clock time the request was received by the proxy.

method.description: JSON-RPC method, e.g. tools/call, tools/list, initialize.

tool.description: Denormalized tool name when method is tools/call. Present so matchers and humans can grep without parsing params.

response.description: Exactly one of result or error.

transport.description: Transport-level detail useful for debugging; never used for matching.

sse.description: True if the response arrived over an SSE stream rather than a plain JSON body.

orphan.description: A request that never received a response before the session ended (timeout, cancellation, disconnect). Recorded for honesty; replay policy for orphans is defined by the stage, not the cassette.
